title: Preferential Trade Agreement between the Republic of Turkey and the Republic of Uzbekistan is approved
bodyHtml:
The "Preferential Trade Agreement between the Republic of Turkey and the Republic of Uzbekistan" signed in Tashkent on March 29, 2022, has been approved with the publication of Law No. 7443. The agreement will enter into force following notifications from both countries that their internal approval processes have been completed.

Under the agreement, Uzbekistan will provide concessions to Turkey on 12 industrial products. These products include flat steel products made of iron and steel, centrifugal pumps, pipes-boilers-tanks, devices generating electricity from diesel, transformers, electrical panels, some machinery components and parts, electrical conductors, control devices, and prefabricated structures. Uzbekistan will offer a 60 percent reduction in customs duties for prefabricated structures and a 50 percent reduction for other goods.

Turkey will provide Uzbekistan with customs duty reductions on 12 agricultural products subject to quantity limitations that have limited production in Turkey and are import dependent. These products include onions, chickpeas, certain vegetable preserves (gherkins, capers, sweet corn), dried peas, black beans of the mango type, white beans, almonds, walnuts, dried black grapes, dried plums and peaches, peppers, and peanuts.

In this context, while Uzbekistan reduces customs duties on some industrial products, Turkey provides market access advantages under quota for a limited number of agricultural products.

To benefit from the preferential regime, the value of foreign inputs used in the manufacture of products should not exceed 20 percent of the EXW price of the goods classified under codes 841370, 850213, and 940690 and 40 percent for other products.

 EUR.1 Movement Certificates will be used in the trade under the agreement, which also allows bilateral cumulation of origin.

Click here for the scope of goods and the text of the relevant agreement submitted to the Turkish Grand National Assembly.
